task_completion.description: Load the cook_county_daily.csv dataset into Epi Info 7
task_completion.visual_evidence: Epi Info 7 Visual Dashboard is open and the data source indicator (usually at the top of the dashboard) shows that 'cook_county_daily.csv' has been loaded.

task_completion.description: Add the Aberration Detection Analysis gadget to the dashboard
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Aberration Detection' gadget panel or configuration window is visible on the Epi Info 7 dashboard canvas.

task_completion.description: Configure the gadget with the correct date and count fields
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Aberration Detection gadget's properties/configuration panel, the Date field dropdown is set to 'date' and the Main/Count field dropdown is set to 'cases'.

task_completion.description: Configure the gadget with the C2 method and 7-day lag time
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Aberration Detection gadget's properties/configuration panel, the Method is set to 'C2' (or 'C2-MEDIUM') and the Lag Time (or baseline days) is set to 7.

task_completion.description: Generate and display the outbreak detection results
task_completion.visual_evidence: The gadget displays a generated chart or data table showing the aberration detection results (e.g., a time series chart with flags for potential outbreaks).

task_completion.description: Save the dashboard canvas to the specified Surveillance folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog shows the file being saved as 'DailyMonitoring' in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\Surveillance\', or the main Epi Info 7 window title bar reflects the saved filename 'DailyMonitoring'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Epi Info 7 GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Epi Info 7 application interface being actively clicked and configured, rather than a terminal window executing a script.

integrity.description: Verify the chart/results are natively generated by Epi Info 7
integrity.visual_evidence: The aberration detection chart appears embedded within the Epi Info 7 gadget UI, not as a standalone generic image viewer or a Python matplotlib plot.

integrity.description: Verify the canvas file was saved via the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The save action is performed through the Epi Info 7 'Save Canvas' functionality, rather than creating a dummy text file named 'DailyMonitoring.canvas' in a file explorer or terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct tool in Epi Info 7 is the 'Aberration Detection Analysis gadget'. The requested 'C2' method corresponds to 'C2-MEDIUM' in the software. The dataset uses 'date' and 'cases' fields. Note: Epi Info 7 natively saves dashboard canvases with a '.cvs7' extension, so the saved file might appear as DailyMonitoring.cvs7 or DailyMonitoring.canvas.cvs7 depending on how the agent typed it; either is acceptable as long as the base name and location are correct.
